member.name: T:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine

member.name: E:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.DeviceStarted
member.summary: Occurs when an audio device starts processing audio. This can be used by external components (like a MIDI backend) to send synchronization messages (e.g., MIDI Start).

member.name: E:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.DeviceStopped
member.summary: Occurs when an audio device stops processing audio. This can be used by external components to send synchronization messages (e.g., MIDI Stop).

member.name: E:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.AudioFramesRendered
member.summary: Occurs after a block of audio has been prepared for rendering by a playback device. This event provides a sample-accurate clock tick for driving master synchronization (e.g., MIDI Clock).

member.summary: For internal use by audio device implementations. Raises the event.

member.summary: For internal use by audio device implementations. Raises the event using a reusable EventArgs object to avoid allocation overhead in the hot path.
member.param: The cached event arguments object.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.UseMidiBackend(SoundFlow.Interfaces.IMidiBackend)
member.summary: Configures the engine to use a specific MIDI backend implementation. This method must be called before any MIDI operations are performed.

member.exception: Thrown if a MIDI backend has already been configured.

member.summary: Unsolos the specified sound component.

member.summary: Gets the currently soloed component, if any.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.RegisterCodecFactory(SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory)
member.summary: Registers a codec factory with the audio engine using its default priority.
member.param: The codec factory to register.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.UnregisterCodecFactory(System.String)
member.summary: Unregisters a codec factory from the engine using its unique ID.
member.param: The unique ID of the factory to unregister.
member.returns: true if the factory was found and removed; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.SetCodecPriority(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets a new priority for a registered codec factory, overriding its default priority. This allows for runtime prioritization of codecs.
member.param: The unique ID of the factory to prioritize.
The new priority value. Higher numbers are tried first.
member.returns: true if the factory was found and its priority was updated; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.GetRegisteredCodecs(System.String)
member.summary: Gets a read-only list of all registered codec factories for a specific format, ordered from highest to lowest priority.
member.param: The format identifier (e.g., "flac").
member.returns: A read-only list of factories, or an empty list if none are registered.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.CreateEncoder(System.IO.Stream,System.String,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Constructs a sound encoder for the specified format. It queries registered codec factories based on priority to find a suitable implementation.
member.param: The stream to write encoded audio to.
The string identifier for the desired audio format (e.g., "wav", "flac").
The audio format of the source PCM data.
member.returns: An instance of a sound encoder.
member.exception: Thrown if no registered and working codec factory is found for the specified format.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.CreateDecoder(System.IO.Stream,System.String,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Constructs a sound decoder for the specified format. It queries registered codec factories based on priority to find a suitable implementation.
member.param: The stream containing the audio data.
The string identifier for the audio format (e.g., "mp3", "flac").
The audio format containing channels, sample rate, and sample format.
member.returns: An instance of a sound decoder.
member.exception: Thrown if no registered and working codec factory is found for the specified format.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.CreateDecoder(System.IO.Stream,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat@,System.Nullable{SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat})
member.summary: Constructs a sound decoder by probing the stream with all registered codec factories. This method is used when the audio format is not known beforehand. Factories are tried in order of priority.
member.param: The stream containing the audio data. Must be readable and seekable.
When this method returns, contains the audio format detected by the successful decoder.
An optional hint for the desired output audio format. The factory should attempt to produce this format if possible.
member.returns: An instance of a sound decoder.
member.exception: Thrown if no registered codec factory could decode the provided stream.

The audio format to use for both devices.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.SwitchDevice(SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.AudioPlaybackDevice,SoundFlow.Structs.DeviceInfo,SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.DeviceConfig)
member.summary: Switches an active playback device to a new physical device, preserving its audio graph. The old device instance will be disposed.
member.param: The playback device instance to replace.
The info for the new physical device to use.
Optional configuration for the new device.
member.returns: A new, active instance.

member.summary: Switches an active capture device to a new physical device, preserving its event subscribers. The old device instance will be disposed.
member.param: The capture device instance to replace.
The info for the new physical device to use.
Optional configuration for the new device.
member.returns: A new, active instance.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine.SwitchDevice(SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.FullDuplexDevice,System.Nullable{SoundFlow.Structs.DeviceInfo},System.Nullable{SoundFlow.Structs.DeviceInfo},SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.DeviceConfig)
member.summary: Switches the devices used by a full-duplex instance, preserving its state. The old duplex device instance will be disposed.
member.param: The full-duplex device instance to replace.
Info for the new playback device. If null, the existing playback device is used.
Info for the new capture device. If null, the existing capture device is used.
Optional configuration for the new device(s).
member.returns: A new, active instance.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioCaptureDevice.ProcessAudioCallback(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDevice)
member.summary: The callback method invoked by the MiniAudio backend when captured audio data is available. This method converts the raw device data to the standard 32-bit float format and raises the event.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig
member.summary: Represents detailed configuration for a MiniAudio device, allowing fine-grained control over general and backend-specific settings.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.PeriodSizeInFrames
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired period size in frames per channel. Takes precedence over PeriodSizeInMilliseconds. Set to 0 to use the backend's default.
member.example: 10ms * 48khz = 480 frames, If channels are set to 2, then It will automatically be 960 frames

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.PeriodSizeInMilliseconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired period size in milliseconds per channel. Set to 0 to use the backend's default.
member.example: 10ms, If channels are set to 2, then It will automatically be 20ms, asking the player for a larger buffer

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.Periods
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of periods to use for the device's buffer. Set to 0 to use the backend's default.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.NoPreSilencedOutputBuffer
member.summary: When set to true, the contents of the output buffer passed into the data callback will be left undefined rather than initialized to silence.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.NoClip
member.summary: When set to true, the contents of the output buffer passed into the data callback will not be clipped after returning. Only applies when the playback sample format is F32.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.NoDisableDenormals
member.summary: When set to true, the backend will not attempt to disable denormal floating point numbers, which can improve performance at the risk of precision loss.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.NoFixedSizedCallback
member.summary: Disables strict fixed-sized data callbacks. Setting this to true will result in the period size being treated only as a hint to the backend. This is an optimization for those who don't need fixed sized callbacks.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.Playback
member.summary: Gets or sets the configuration specific to playback.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.Capture
member.summary: Gets or sets the configuration specific to capture.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.Wasapi
member.summary: Gets or sets the configuration specific to the WASAPI backend. This is only used on Windows.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.MiniAudioDeviceConfig.CoreAudio

member.summary: Contains settings for a specific direction (playback or capture).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.DeviceSubConfig.ShareMode
member.summary: The sharing mode for the device. Use Exclusive for lowest latency if available.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.DeviceSubConfig.IsLoopback

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings
member.summary: Contains settings specific to the WASAPI audio backend on Windows.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings.Usage
member.summary: Gets or sets the usage scenario for the audio stream. This hints to the operating system about the stream's purpose, potentially affecting system-level prioritization and processing. Maps to `ma_wasapi_usage` in MiniAudio's `ma_device_config.wasapi.usage`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings.NoAutoConvertSRC
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to disable automatic sample rate conversion (SRC) by WASAPI. When true, MiniAudio will perform the SRC instead of the OS. Maps to `ma_device_config.wasapi.noAutoConvertSRC`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings.NoDefaultQualitySRC
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to disable the use of `AUDCLNT_STREAMFLAGS_SRC_DEFAULT_QUALITY`. When true, WASAPI will not use its default SRC quality. Maps to `ma_device_config.wasapi.noDefaultQualitySRC`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings.NoAutoStreamRouting
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to disable automatic stream routing by WASAPI. Maps to `ma_device_config.wasapi.noAutoStreamRouting`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.WasapiSettings.NoHardwareOffloading
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to disable WASAPI's hardware offloading feature. Maps to `ma_device_config.wasapi.noHardwareOffloading`.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.CoreAudioSettings
member.summary: Contains settings specific to the CoreAudio backend on macOS and iOS.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.CoreAudioSettings.AllowNominalSampleRateChange
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to allow CoreAudio to change the sample rate at the operating system level. This setting is typically for desktop macOS. Maps to `ma_device_config.coreaudio.allowNominalSampleRateChange`.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.AlsaSettings
member.summary: Contains settings specific to the ALSA audio backend on Linux.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.AlsaSettings.NoMMap
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to disable Memory Map (MMap) mode for ALSA. Maps to `ma_device_config.alsa.noMMap`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.AlsaSettings.NoAutoFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to open the ALSA device with `SND_PCM_NO_AUTO_FORMAT`. This disables automatic format conversion by ALSA. Maps to `ma_device_config.alsa.noAutoFormat`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.AlsaSettings.NoAutoChannels
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to open the ALSA device with `SND_PCM_NO_AUTO_CHANNELS`. This disables automatic channel count conversion by ALSA. Maps to `ma_device_config.alsa.noAutoChannels`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.AlsaSettings.NoAutoResample
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to open the ALSA device with `SND_PCM_NO_AUTO_RESAMPLE`. This disables automatic resampling by ALSA. Maps to `ma_device_config.alsa.noAutoResample`.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Backends.MiniAudio.Devices.PulseSettings.StreamNameCapture

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator
member.summary: Generates an envelope signal, commonly used for controlling the amplitude of audio signals over time.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState
member.summary: Defines the different stages of the envelope.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState.Idle
member.summary: The envelope is inactive and outputting silence.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState.Attack
member.summary: The envelope is in the attack stage, rising from silence to full level.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState.Decay
member.summary: The envelope is in the decay stage, falling from full level to the sustain level.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState.Sustain
member.summary: The envelope is in the sustain stage, holding at a constant level.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.EnvelopeState.Release
member.summary: The envelope is in the release stage, falling from the sustain level to silence.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerMode
member.summary: Defines how the envelope is triggered and how it transitions through its stages.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerMode.NoteOn
member.summary: The envelope enters the release stage only when is called after a . Suitable for note-on/note-off style triggering.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerMode.Gate
member.summary: The envelope enters the release stage immediately when is called. Suitable for gate-style triggering where the envelope follows the gate signal.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerMode.Trigger
member.summary: The envelope performs an instant attack and decay upon , bypassing the sustain stage. Useful for percussive sounds or short bursts.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The parent audio engine.
The audio format containing channels and sample rate and sample format

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.State
member.summary: Gets the current stage of the envelope.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.AttackTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the attack time in seconds. This is the time it takes for the envelope to rise from 0 to 1.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.DecayTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the decay time in seconds. This is the time it takes for the envelope to fall from 1 to the .

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.SustainLevel
member.summary: Gets or sets the sustain level, a value between 0 and 1. This is the level the envelope will hold at after the decay stage and before the release stage (in NoteOn mode).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.ReleaseTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the release time in seconds. This is the time it takes for the envelope to fall from the current level to 0.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.Trigger
member.summary: Gets or sets the trigger mode, determining how the envelope reacts to trigger signals. See for available modes.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.Retrigger
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the envelope should retrigger if is called while it's already active. If true, calling will restart the envelope from the attack stage even if it's currently in another stage.

member.name: E:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.LevelChanged
member.summary: Occurs when the envelope level changes during audio generation. Subscribers can use this event to react to envelope level changes, for example, to visualize the envelope shape.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerOn
member.summary: Triggers the envelope to start the attack stage. This method is typically called when a note is pressed or a gate signal is activated.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.TriggerOff
member.summary: Triggers the envelope to start the release stage. This method is typically called when a note is released or a gate signal is deactivated.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.CalculateRates
member.summary: Calculates the rate of level change per sample for attack, decay, and release stages based on the current parameters and sample rate. This method is called internally when the envelope state changes to ensure correct rate calculations.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.ProcessSample
member.summary: Processes and returns a single sample of the envelope's output. This is more efficient for per-sample modulation than filling a buffer.
member.returns: The current envelope level for a single sample.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.EnvelopeGenerator.Update
member.summary: Updates the envelope level based on the current state and calculated rates. This method is called per sample to advance the envelope through its stages.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator
member.summary: A Low Frequency Oscillator (LFO) component that generates periodic waveforms at a sub-audio rate.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The parent audio engine.
The audio format containing channels and sample rate and sample format

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType
member.summary: Defines the different waveform shapes that the LFO can generate.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.Sine
member.summary: A smooth, sinusoidal waveform.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.Square
member.summary: A waveform that alternates abruptly between two levels, creating a pulse.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.Triangle
member.summary: A waveform that linearly ramps up and down, creating a triangular shape.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.Sawtooth
member.summary: A waveform that ramps upwards linearly and then abruptly resets to the starting level.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.ReverseSawtooth
member.summary: A waveform that ramps downwards linearly and then abruptly resets to the starting level.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.Random
member.summary: A waveform that generates random values at each sample.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.WaveformType.SampleAndHold
member.summary: A waveform that holds a random value constant for a cycle and then changes to a new random value.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.TriggerMode
member.summary: Defines how the LFO's phase is triggered or progresses.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.TriggerMode.FreeRunning
member.summary: The LFO runs continuously without any external triggering.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.TriggerMode.NoteTrigger
member.summary: The LFO's phase is reset to the value each time the method is called.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Rate
member.summary: Gets or sets the rate of the LFO in Hertz (cycles per second). This determines how fast the waveform oscillates. Must be a non-negative value.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Depth
member.summary: Gets or sets the depth or amplitude of the LFO output, typically in the range of 0 to 1 or beyond for more pronounced effects. This scales the output waveform.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of waveform generated by the LFO. See for available waveform shapes.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Mode
member.summary: Gets or sets the trigger mode of the LFO, determining how its phase is controlled. See for available trigger modes.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Phase
member.summary: Gets or sets the phase offset of the LFO in radians. This allows for shifting the starting point of the waveform.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.OnOutputChanged
member.summary: An event that is invoked whenever the LFO's output value changes during audio generation. Subscribers can use this to react to changes in the LFO's output, for example, to drive visualisations or other components.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.Trigger
member.summary: Triggers the LFO to reset its phase if in mode. For Sample and Hold waveform, it also updates the held random value. This method has no effect if the LFO is in mode.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.GenerateSample
member.summary: Generates a single sample of the LFO waveform based on the current parameters and internal state.
member.returns: The generated LFO sample value.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.LowFrequencyOscillator.GetLastOutput
member.summary: Gets the last generated output value of the LFO.
member.returns: The last output value.

member.summary: Checks if adding a component to this mixer would create a cycle in the audio graph.

member.summary: Removes a sound component from the mixer.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Mixer.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the mixer and all its components.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator
member.summary: Generates various types of high-quality, band-limited audio waveforms at a specified frequency and amplitude.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType
member.summary: Defines the different types of waveforms the oscillator can generate.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Sine
member.summary: A pure sine wave, known for its smooth and fundamental tone.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Square
member.summary: A band-limited square wave, rich in odd harmonics, producing a bright, clean sound without digital aliasing.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Sawtooth
member.summary: A band-limited sawtooth wave, containing both even and odd harmonics, resulting in a bright, clean sound without digital aliasing.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Triangle
member.summary: A triangle wave, containing only odd harmonics. Note: This implementation is not band-limited, but aliasing is less pronounced than with square or saw waves.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Noise
member.summary: Generates random noise across all frequencies, useful for creating percussive sounds or textures.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.WaveformType.Pulse
member.summary: A band-limited pulse wave (also known as a rectangular wave), similar to a square wave but with adjustable pulse width for timbral variations.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, which generates audio waveforms at a specified frequency and amplitude.
member.param: The parent audio engine.
The audio format containing channels and sample rate and sample format

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.Frequency
member.summary: Gets or sets the frequency of the oscillator in Hertz. This determines the pitch of the generated sound.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.Amplitude
member.summary: Gets or sets the amplitude of the oscillator, controlling the loudness of the generated sound. Typically ranges from 0 to 1, but can exceed 1 for overdrive effects if used in later processing stages.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of waveform the oscillator will generate. See for available waveform options.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.PhaseOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the phase offset of the waveform in radians (from 0 to 2*PI). This can be used to synchronize multiple oscillators or create stereo effects.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.PulseWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the pulse width for the waveform, as a fraction of the cycle (0 to 1). A value of 0.5 results in a square wave. This parameter is only effective when is set to .

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.GenerateSample
member.summary: Generates a single audio sample based on the current waveform type, phase, and amplitude. This method updates the internal phase for the next sample.
member.returns: The generated audio sample value.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Oscillator.Polyblep(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Calculates the polynomial correction for a band-limited step function (PolyBLEP). This is used to remove aliasing from waveforms with sharp discontinuities.
member.param: The normalized phase of the oscillator (0 to 1).
The normalized phase increment per sample.
member.returns: The correction value to be subtracted from the naive waveform.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder
member.summary: Component for recording audio data from a capture device, either to a stream or via a callback. Supports various sample and encoding formats and can integrate with and components for real-time processing and analysis during recording. Implements the interface to ensure resources are released properly.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.State
member.summary: Gets the current playback state of the recorder.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.SampleFormat
member.summary: Gets the sample format used for recording.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.FormatId
member.summary: Gets the format identifier (e.g., "wav", "flac") used for encoding.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.SampleRate
member.summary: Gets the sample rate used for recording, in samples per second.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.Channels
member.summary: Gets the number of channels being recorded (e.g., 1 for mono, 2 for stereo).

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.Stream
member.summary: Gets the file path where audio will be recorded, if recording to a file. Will be an empty string if recording via a callback.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.FilePath
member.summary: Gets the final destination file path where audio will be recorded. Will be null if recording via a callback or stream.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.ProcessCallback
member.summary: Gets or sets the callback function to be invoked when audio data is processed. This is used when recording directly to memory or for custom processing, instead of to a file.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.AudioCaptureDevice,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class to record audio to a file.
member.param: The capture device to record from.
The final destination path for the recorded audio file.
The string identifier for the desired encoding format (e.g., "wav", "flac"). Defaults to "wav".

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.AudioCaptureDevice,System.IO.Stream,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class to record audio to a stream.
member.param: The capture device to record from.
The stream to write encoded recorded audio to.
The string identifier for the desired encoding format (e.g., "wav", "flac"). Defaults to "wav".

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.#ctor(SoundFlow.Abstracts.Devices.AudioCaptureDevice,SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioProcessCallback)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class to record audio and process it via a callback function.
member.param: The capture device to record from.
The callback function to be invoked when audio data is processed. This function should handle the recorded audio data.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets a read-only list of components applied to the recorder.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets a read-only list of components applied to the recorder.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.StartRecording(SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.SoundTags)
member.summary: Starts the audio recording process. If recording to a file or stream, it initializes an audio encoder.
member.param: Optional metadata tags to write to the file upon completion of the recording.
member.exception: Thrown if an invalid stream or callback is provided.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.ResumeRecording
member.summary: Resumes recording from a paused state. Has no effect if the recorder is not in the state.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.PauseRecording
member.summary: Pauses the recording process. Audio data is no longer processed or encoded until recording is resumed. Has no effect if the recorder is not in the state.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.StopRecordingAsync
member.summary: Stops the recording process and releases resources. If recording to a file, it finalizes the encoding process and writes metadata tags if provided.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.StopRecording
member.summary: Synchronously stops the recording process.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.AddModifier(SoundFlow.Abstracts.SoundModifier)
member.summary: Adds a to the recording pipeline. Modifiers are applied to the audio data before encoding or processing via callback.
member.param: The modifier to add.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.RemoveModifier(SoundFlow.Abstracts.SoundModifier)
member.summary: Removes a from the recording pipeline.
member.param: The modifier to remove.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.AddAnalyzer(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioAnalyzer)
member.summary: Adds an to the recording pipeline. Analyzers can be used to process and extract data from the audio during recording.
member.param: The analyzer to add.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.RemoveAnalyzer(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioAnalyzer)
member.summary: Removes an from the recording pipeline.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.Recorder.OnAudioProcessed(System.Span{System.Single},SoundFlow.Enums.Capability)
member.summary: Handles the audio processed event from the audio engine. This method is invoked by the audio engine when new audio samples are available. It processes the samples through the added and components, checks the current state, invokes the (if set), and encodes the samples using the (if recording to a file).

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector
member.summary: Detects voice activity in audio streams using spectral analysis. This version includes hysteresis (hangover) to prevent rapid state changes.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.IsVoiceActive
member.summary: Gets whether voice activity is currently detected.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.EnergyThreshold
member.summary: Gets or sets the energy threshold for voice detection.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.ActivationTimeMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the time in milliseconds the signal must be considered speech before activation. Helps prevent short noise bursts from triggering the VAD.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.HangoverTimeMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the time in milliseconds to keep the VAD active after the last speech frame. Prevents the VAD from deactivating during short pauses.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.SpeechLowBand
member.summary: Gets or sets the lower bound of the frequency range used for speech detection in Hz.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.SpeechHighBand
member.summary: Gets or sets the upper bound of the frequency range used for speech detection in Hz.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.#ctor(SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat,System.Int32,System.Single,SoundFlow.Interfaces.IVisualizer)
member.summary: Initializes a new voice activity detector.
member.param: The audio format containing channels and sample rate and sample format
FFT window size (must be power of two). A common value is 1024 for 48kHz audio.
Detection energy threshold. This is highly dependent on your input signal level.
Optional visualizer for debugging
member.remarks: Increase FFT size for better frequency resolution. Decrease threshold for higher sensitivity. Use larger FFT sizes in low-noise environments. Calibrate threshold based on input levels.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.Analyze(System.Span{System.Single},System.Int32)
member.summary: Analyzes audio buffer for voice activity.

member.name: E:SoundFlow.Components.VoiceActivityDetector.SpeechDetected
member.summary: Occurs when voice activity state changes.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher
member.summary: Implements the WSOLA (Waveform Similarity Overlap-Add) algorithm for real-time time stretching and pitch preservation of audio. It allows changing playback speed without altering pitch.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The initial number of audio channels. Defaults to 2 if not positive.
The initial playback speed. Defaults to 1.0f.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.SetChannels(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the number of audio channels for the time stretcher. Reinitializes internal buffers if channels change.
member.param: The number of audio channels.
member.exception: Thrown if channels is not positive.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.SetSpeed(System.Single)
member.summary: Sets the playback speed for time stretching.
member.param: The desired playback speed (e.g., 0.5 for half speed, 2.0 for double speed).
member.exception: Thrown if speed is not positive.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.MinInputSamplesToProcess
member.summary: Gets the minimum number of input samples required in the internal buffer to perform a processing step.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.ResetState
member.summary: Resets the internal state of the time stretcher, clearing all buffers and flags. This should be called when seeking or stopping playback.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.Reset
member.summary: Resets the internal state of the time stretcher, clearing all buffers and flags.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.GetTargetSpeed
member.summary: Gets the current target playback speed set for the time stretcher.
member.returns: The current playback speed.

member.summary: Processes a segment of audio data for time stretching.
member.param: The input audio data to be processed.
The span to write the processed audio data to.
Output parameter: The number of samples consumed from the input span.
Output parameter: The number of *original* source samples that the generated output represents.
member.returns: The number of samples written to the output span.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Components.WsolaTimeStretcher.Flush(System.Span{System.Single})
member.summary: Flushes any remaining buffered audio data through the time stretcher. This is typically called at the end of a stream to ensure all data is processed.

member.returns: The total number of samples written to the output span during flushing.

A flag indicating whether this segment is responsible for disposing the . Set to true if this segment is the primary owner of the provider; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.
Thrown if or is negative, or if is zero or negative.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.InitializeWsolaBuffers
member.summary: Initializes the internal buffers used for WSOLA time stretching.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.FullResetState
member.summary: Resets internal state variables used during ReadProcessedSamples. Crucially, this should be called if seeking occurs externally or if parameters change that affect reading.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.ResetForNewSourcePassForWsola
member.summary: Resets the internal state for WSOLA when transitioning to a new source loop pass. This ensures WSOLA processes a fresh segment of the source audio.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.InvalidateReadState
member.summary: Marks the internal read state as invalid, forcing the next read operation to perform a seek on the underlying data provider. This is called by the Composition after a user-initiated seek.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.StretchedSourceDuration
member.summary: Gets the duration of the segment's core content after pitch-preserved time stretching has been applied. This does not yet account for SpeedFactor.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.EffectiveDurationOnTimeline
member.summary: Gets the effective duration of a single instance of this segment on the timeline, considering the . Loop settings are not directly included here as they determine repetitions, not the base duration of one instance.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.TimelineEndTime
member.summary: Gets the end time of this segment on the overall composition timeline, considering its start time and total looped duration.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment.GetTotalLoopedDurationOnTimeline
member.summary: Calculates the total effective duration of this segment on the timeline, taking into account its settings ( or ).
member.returns: A representing the total duration on the timeline.

member.summary: Gets or sets the parent that owns these settings. This is used internally to propagate dirty state.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets the chain of sound modifiers (effects) to be applied to this segment. Modifiers are applied in the order they appear in this list.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets the chain of audio analyzers to process this segment's audio. Analyzers process the audio after all segment modifiers have been applied.

member.summary: Gets or sets the volume level of the segment. A value of 1.0f is normal volume.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.Pan
member.summary: Gets or sets the stereo pan position of the segment. A value of -1.0f means full left, 0.0f means center, and 1.0f means full right.

member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of the fade-in effect at the beginning of the segment. If set to TimeSpan.Zero, no fade-in is applied.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.FadeInCurve
member.summary: Gets or sets the curve type used for the fade-in effect.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.FadeOutDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of the fade-out effect at the end of the segment. If set to TimeSpan.Zero, no fade-out is applied.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.FadeOutCurve
member.summary: Gets or sets the curve type used for the fade-out effect.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.IsReversed
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the segment's audio should be played in reverse.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.Loop
member.summary: Gets or sets the looping settings for the segment, defining how it repeats.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.SpeedFactor
member.summary: Playback speed factor (affects pitch and tempo). 1.0 is normal.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.TimeStretchFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the time stretch factor for pitch-preserved stretching. 1.0 = no stretch, more than 1.0 = longer duration, less than 1.0 = shorter duration. If is set, this factor is derived from it.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings.TargetStretchDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the target duration for pitch-preserved time stretching. If set, this overrides . Set to null to use instead.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionEditor.SilenceSegment(SoundFlow.Editing.Track,System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary:
Silences a specified time range on a given track by manipulating existing segments and inserting a new silent segment. This operation is non-destructive to original sources. It may:
Remove segments fully contained within the silence range.
Trim segments that partially overlap the start or end of the silence range.
Split segments that span across the entire silence range into two, before and after the silenced part.
An explicit silent audio segment is then inserted to cover the specified range. The overall timing of other audio on the track (outside the silenced range) remains unchanged.
member.param: The track to apply the silence to.
The start time of the range to silence on the track.
The duration of the silence.
member.returns: The newly created silent that fills the silenced range, or null if is zero or negative.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionEditor.InsertSegment(SoundFlow.Editing.Track,System.TimeSpan,SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Inserts an into a specified track at a given insertion point.
member.param: The track into which the segment should be inserted.
The timeline point at which to insert the segment.
The audio segment to insert.
If true, all segments on the track that start at or after the insertion point will be shifted later by the duration of the inserted segment.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.RecordingMode
member.summary: Defines the behavior of the MIDI recorder when recording starts.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.RecordingMode.Normal
member.summary: Always creates a new MIDI segment for the recording.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.RecordingMode.OverdubMerge
member.summary: Merges the newly recorded notes into an existing MIDI segment. If no segment is at the start time, creates a new one.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder
member.summary: Manages the MIDI recording workflow for a . This class handles arming tracks, starting/stopping recording, and updating the sample clock.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.PunchInTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the time at which recording should automatically start. If null, recording starts immediately when is called.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.PunchOutTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the time at which recording should automatically stop. If null, recording continues until manually stopped.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.IsRecording
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether any armed tracks are currently recording.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.IsWaitingForPunchIn
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the recorder is armed and waiting for the punch-in time to be reached.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.#ctor(SoundFlow.Editing.Composition)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The composition instance that will be recorded into.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.ArmTrackForRecording(SoundFlow.Editing.MidiTrack,SoundFlow.Midi.Devices.MidiInputDevice)
member.summary: Arms a MIDI track for recording from a specific input device.
member.param: The MIDI track to arm.
The MIDI input device to record from.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.DisarmTrack(SoundFlow.Editing.MidiTrack)
member.summary: Disarms a MIDI track, stopping any active recording on it.
member.param: The MIDI track to disarm.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.StartRecording(System.TimeSpan,SoundFlow.Editing.RecordingMode,SoundFlow.Editing.MidiSegment)
member.summary: Pre-arms the recorder. If a is set, it will wait until that time to start. Otherwise, it starts immediately.
member.param: The time on the composition timeline where recording begins if not using punch-in.
The recording mode to use (e.g., create new segment or overdub).
For overdub/merge mode, the segment to record into. Ignored for normal mode.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.StartInternalRecorders(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Immediately starts all armed recorders. For internal use by the transport.
member.param: The exact start time.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.StopRecording
member.summary: Stops recording on all currently active MIDI recorders.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.UpdateSampleClock(System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates the internal sample clock for all active recorders. This method must be called from a synchronized audio context, such as the composition's render loop.
member.param: The number of samples processed in the last audio block.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.OnTransportLoop(System.Int64)
member.summary: Notifies active recorders that a transport loop has occurred. This is used to correctly timestamp notes in loop recording sessions.
member.param: The duration of the loop in audio samples.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRecorder.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes all active MIDI recorders, effectively disarming all tracks.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer
member.summary: Renders a into an audio stream. This class implements , allowing a whole composition to be treated as a single audio source for playback or further processing.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.IsSyncDriven
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the renderer's transport is driven by an external sync source. When true, the internal clock is bypassed, and playback is advanced via `AdvanceBySyncTicks`.
member.remarks: This property shouldn't be set manually. It is managed by the audio engine. It's exposed as public to be used in external backend implementations.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.LoopStartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the start time of the playback loop. If null, looping is disabled.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.LoopEndTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the end time of the playback loop. If null, looping is disabled.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.#ctor(SoundFlow.Editing.Composition)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The composition instance to be rendered.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.SyncPlay
member.summary: Starts playback from the beginning.
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.SyncStop
member.summary: Stops playback and resets the position to the beginning.
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.SyncContinue
member.summary: Resumes playback from the current position.
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.SyncSeek(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Seeks the playback position to a specific time.
member.param: The time to seek to.
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.AdvanceBySyncTicks(System.Int32)
member.summary: Advances the playback position by a specified number of sync ticks. This is used when is true.
member.param: The number of ticks to advance.
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.GetTempoAtCurrentPosition
member.summary: Gets the tempo marker active at the current playback position.
member.returns: The active .
member.remarks: This method is intended for use by external audio backend implementations to control a sync-driven transport and should not be called directly by the user.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.Render
member.summary: Renders the full composition into a new float array.
member.returns: A float array containing the rendered audio samples. An empty array is returned if no samples are rendered.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.Render(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Renders a specific time portion of the composition into a new float array.
member.param: The global timeline start time of the portion to render.
The duration of the audio to render.
member.returns: A float array containing the rendered audio samples. An empty array is returned if no samples are rendered.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.Render(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.Span{System.Single})
member.summary: Renders a specific time portion of the composition into a provided buffer. This method mixes audio from all active tracks, applies master volume, and performs clipping.
member.param: The global timeline start time of the portion to render.
The duration of the audio to render.
The span to fill with rendered audio samples. This buffer will be cleared before rendering.
member.returns: The number of samples actually written to the output buffer.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.GetActiveTracksForRendering
member.summary: Determines which tracks are active for rendering based on their mute, solo, and enabled states. If any track is soloed, only soloed tracks are returned. Otherwise, all non-muted, enabled tracks are returned.
member.returns: A list of objects that should be included in the rendering process.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.CompositionRenderer.CurrentTime
member.summary: Gets the current playback time in .

member.summary: Gets a unique identifier for this control point instance. This is used to reliably track the point during editing operations.

member.summary: Gets or sets the absolute time of the control point in MIDI ticks from the beginning of the sequence.

member.summary: Gets or sets the value of the control point. For CC messages, this is 0-127. For Pitch Bend, this is a 14-bit value from 0-16383 (centered at 8192).

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

The MIDI value for the control point.

member.summary: A data structure describing a modification to be applied to a . Null properties indicate no change for that attribute.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.ControlPointModification.PointId
member.summary: The unique identifier of the control point to modify.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.ControlPointModification.NewTick
member.summary: The new absolute time in ticks, if it is to be changed.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.ControlPointModification.NewValue
member.summary: The new value, if it is to be changed.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.FadeCurveType
member.summary: Defines the types of curves that can be used for fade-in and fade-out effects on audio segments.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.FadeCurveType.Linear
member.summary: A linear fade, where the volume changes at a constant rate.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.FadeCurveType.Logarithmic
member.summary: A logarithmic fade, where the volume changes more rapidly at the beginning/end of the fade and more slowly in the middle, to better match human perception of loudness.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.FadeCurveType.SCurve
member.summary: An S-shaped fade curve, providing a smooth transition in and out of the fade. Also known as EaseInOutSine or Smoothstep.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.LoopSettings
member.summary: Defines the looping behavior for an .

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.LoopSettings.Repetitions
member.summary: Number of times the segment should repeat after its initial play. 0 means play once, 1 means play twice total, etc. MaxValue for infinite looping until target duration is met.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.LoopSettings.TargetDuration
member.summary: If specified, the segment will loop to fill this total duration, potentially cutting off the last loop partway. If null, Repetitions will be used.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.LoopSettings.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The number of times the segment should repeat after its initial play. Clamped to be non-negative.
The total duration the segment should loop to fill. If null, will be used.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.LoopSettings.PlayOnce
member.summary: Gets a instance configured for playing the segment once (no repetitions).

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType
member.summary: Defines the type of MIDI message that can be used as a mapping source.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType.ControlChange
member.summary: A standard MIDI Control Change message (CC 0-127).

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType.NoteOn
member.summary: A MIDI Note On message, typically used for triggers or velocity-sensitive controls.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType.NoteOff
member.summary: A MIDI Note Off message, typically used to detect the release of a key/pad.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType.PitchBend
member.summary: A 14-bit Pitch Bend message.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingSourceType.HighResolutionControlChange
member.summary: A high-resolution 14-bit Control Change message, typically implemented using NRPN or RPN.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingBehavior
member.summary: Defines the behavior of the MIDI mapping when triggered.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingBehavior.Absolute
member.summary: The transformed MIDI value directly sets the property value.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingBehavior.Toggle
member.summary: Each trigger flips the current value of a boolean property.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingBehavior.Trigger
member.summary: The mapping triggers a method call.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingBehavior.Relative
member.summary: The incoming MIDI value increments or decrements the current property value.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingTargetType
member.summary: Defines the target type for a MIDI mapping.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingTargetType.Property
member.summary: The mapping targets a public property on the object.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingTargetType.Method
member.summary: The mapping targets a public method on the object.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingArgumentSource
member.summary: Defines the source of a value for a method argument in a Trigger mapping.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingArgumentSource.MidiValue
member.summary: Use the incoming MIDI value (e.g., velocity, CC value).

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingArgumentSource.Constant
member.summary: Use a fixed, constant value defined in the mapping.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingCurveType
member.summary: Defines the transformation curve for mapping a MIDI value to a parameter value.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingCurveType.Linear
member.summary: A straight, 1:1 scaling between the source and target ranges.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingCurveType.Exponential
member.summary: A curve where small changes in the source result in larger changes in the target at the higher end of the range.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingCurveType.Logarithmic
member.summary: A curve where small changes in the source result in larger changes in the target at the lower end of the range.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiInputSource
member.summary: Defines the source of a MIDI mapping, specifying which message will trigger it.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiInputSource.DeviceName
member.summary: The unique name of the MIDI input device.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiInputSource.Channel
member.summary: The MIDI channel (1-16). A value of 0 represents Omni (any channel).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiInputSource.MessageType
member.summary: The type of MIDI message to listen for.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiInputSource.MessageParameter
member.summary: The specific parameter of the message. For CC, this is the controller number (0-127). For Notes, this is the note number (0-127). For High-Resolution CC, this is the 14-bit NRPN/RPN number. For Pitch Bend, this value is ignored.

member.summary: The source of the value for this argument.

member.summary: The constant value to use if the Source is Constant. This should be a JSON-serializable type (string, number, bool).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MethodArgument.Transformer
member.summary: An optional transformer to apply if the Source is MidiValue. If null, the raw MIDI value is used.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.MidiMappingTarget.MethodArguments
member.summary: A list of arguments to be passed if the target is a method. Ignored for properties.

member.summary: The maximum value of the target property's range.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Mapping.ValueTransformer.CurveType

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.CompositionProjectManager
member.summary: Manages the saving and loading of audio composition projects.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.CompositionProjectManager.SaveProjectAsync(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Editing.Composition,System.String,SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions)
member.summary: Saves the given composition to the specified project file path using the specified options.
member.param: The audio engine instance of the composition.
The composition to save.
The full path where the project file will be saved.
Configuration options for the save operation. If null, default options will be used.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.CompositionProjectManager.LoadProjectAsync(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat,System.String)
member.summary: Loads a composition from the specified project file path.
member.param: The audio engine instance for context.
The audio format of the composition. Cannot be null.
The full path of the project file to load.
member.returns: A tuple containing the loaded Composition and a list of missing/unresolved source references.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.CompositionProjectManager.RelinkMissingMedia(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioEngine,SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to relink a missing audio source reference by providing a new file path. If successful, the reference is updated, and a new ISoundDataProvider is resolved. The caller is responsible for updating any AudioSegments in the composition that use this source reference.
member.param: The audio engine instance for context.
The ProjectSourceReference that is currently marked as missing.
The new absolute file path to the audio source.
The base directory of the current project (used for resolving paths).
member.returns: True if relinking was successful and a new ISoundDataProvider was resolved for the reference; otherwise, false. The updated ISoundDataProvider is set on missingSourceReference.ResolvedDataProvider.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings
member.summary: Represents the serializable settings for an audio segment within a project file. This Data Transfer Object (DTO) mirrors the runtime to facilitate persistence, excluding references to live runtime objects and including serializable representations of modifiers and analyzers.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.Volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the volume level of the segment. A value of 1.0f is normal volume.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.Pan
member.summary: Gets or sets the stereo pan position for the segment. A value of -1.0f means full left, 0.0f means center, and 1.0f means full right.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.FadeInDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of the fade-in effect at the beginning of the segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.FadeInCurve
member.summary: Gets or sets the curve type used for the fade-in effect.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.FadeOutDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of the fade-out effect at the end of the segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.FadeOutCurve
member.summary: Gets or sets the curve type used for the fade-out effect.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.IsReversed
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the segment's audio should be played in reverse.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.Loop
member.summary: Gets or sets the looping settings for the segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.SpeedFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the playback speed factor for the segment (affects both pitch and tempo). 1.0 is normal.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the segment is enabled for playback.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.TimeStretchFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the time stretch factor for pitch-preserved stretching. 1.0 means no stretch, more than 1.0 means longer duration, less than 1.0 means shorter duration. This value is overridden if is specified.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.TargetStretchDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the target duration for pitch-preserved time stretching. If this value is set, it takes precedence over . Set to null to use instead.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing sound modifiers applied to this audio segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing audio analyzers applied to this audio segment.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectAudioSegmentSettings.FromRuntime(SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegmentSettings)
member.summary: Creates a new instance from a runtime object. Note: This method maps scalar properties. Modifiers and Analyzers are typically mapped externally (e.g., in ).
member.param: The runtime to convert.
member.returns: A new DTO populated with data from the runtime settings.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData
member.summary: Represents the root data structure for serializing an entire project. This object encapsulates all necessary information to save and load a project, including composition-wide settings, references to audio sources, and the arrangement of tracks.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.ProjectFileVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the project file format. This is used for backward and forward compatibility checks during loading.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.MasterVolume
member.summary: Gets or sets the master volume level for the entire composition. A value of 1.0f is normal volume.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.TargetSampleRate
member.summary: Gets or sets the target sample rate for rendering this composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.TargetChannels
member.summary: Gets or sets the target number of channels for rendering this composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.TicksPerQuarterNote
member.summary: Gets or sets the time division in ticks per quarter note for MIDI data in the project.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.TempoTrack
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of tempo markers defining the composition's tempo track.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.SourceReferences
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of audio source references used in the composition. This list allows for sharing of audio source data between multiple segments and enables media management features such as consolidation and relinking.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.Tracks
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of tracks included in the composition. Each track contains a sequence of audio segments that contribute to the final mix.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.MidiTracks
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of MIDI tracks included in the composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.MidiTargets
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of MIDI-controllable instrument components in the composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing sound modifiers applied to the master composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing sound analyzers applied to the master composition.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectData.MidiMappings
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of serialized MIDI mappings for the composition.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectEffectData
member.summary: Represents the serializable data for a single SoundModifier or AudioAnalyzer instance, including its type and parameter values.

member.summary: Gets or sets the fully qualified assembly name of the SoundModifier or AudioAnalyzer type. Example: "SoundFlow.Modifiers.ParametricEqualizer, SoundFlow, Version=1.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null"

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectEffectData.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this effect/analyzer is currently enabled.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectEffectData.Parameters
member.summary: Gets or sets the JSON representation of the effect's/analyzer's parameters. This allows storing arbitrary parameter sets for different effect types.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectMidiMapping.Transformer

member.summary: Serializable representation of a for project files.

member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the track.

member.summary: Gets or sets the list of s contained within this track.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions
member.summary: Provides a set of configurable options for saving a composition project.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions.ProjectFileVersion
member.summary: The project file version to write into the saved file. It is recommended to use the library's default version unless you have a specific need for version management. The current default is "1.3.0".

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions.ConsolidatedMediaFolderName
member.summary: The name of the subfolder where consolidated media files will be stored, relative to the project file. Default is "Assets".

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions.MaxEmbedSizeBytes
member.summary: The maximum size in bytes for a media source to be embedded directly into the project file as Base64 data. Sources larger than this will be consolidated to a file instead (if consolidation is enabled). Default is 1MB (1 * 1024 * 1024 bytes).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions.ConsolidateMedia
member.summary: If true, external and in-memory/stream audio sources will be copied into the 'ConsolidatedMediaFolderName' to make the project self-contained. Default is true.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSaveOptions.EmbedSmallMedia
member.summary: If true, small audio sources (below 'MaxEmbedSizeBytes') will be embedded directly into the project file, ignoring the 'ConsolidateMedia' setting for those specific sources. Default is true.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment
member.summary: Serializable representation of an for project files.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the audio segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.SourceReferenceId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier linking this segment to its corresponding audio source defined in a .

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.SourceStartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the starting time offset within the raw audio source data from which this segment begins reading.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.SourceDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of audio to read from the raw source data, starting from .

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.TimelineStartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the starting time of this segment on the overall composition timeline.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.Settings
member.summary: Gets or sets the configurable playback and effect settings for this audio segment. These settings control aspects like volume, pan, fades, looping, and speed.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing sound modifiers applied to this segment.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSegment.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of instances representing sound analyzers applied to this segment.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference
member.summary: Represents how an audio source is referenced within a saved project.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.Id
member.summary: A unique identifier for this source within the project.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.OriginalAbsolutePath
member.summary: The original absolute file path of the audio source. This is stored for reference and potential relinking.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.ConsolidatedRelativePath
member.summary: The relative path to the audio file if it's consolidated within the project folder. Null if not consolidated or if embedded.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.IsEmbedded
member.summary: If true, the audio data for this source is embedded directly in the project file.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.EmbeddedDataB64
member.summary: The raw audio data, if IsEmbedded is true. Stored as base64 string for JSON compatibility. This will be null if the data is not embedded.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.IsMidiData
member.summary: If true, this reference points to MIDI data instead of audio data.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.OriginalSampleFormat
member.summary: The original sample format of the source if known, especially important for embedded data.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.OriginalSampleRate
member.summary: The original sample rate of the source if known.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.OriginalChannelLayout
member.summary: The original channel layout of the source audio.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.IsMissing
member.summary: For internal use during loading: flag indicating if the media is currently missing. Not serialized.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.ResolvedDataProvider
member.summary: For internal use during loading: the resolved ISoundDataProvider instance. Not serialized.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectSourceReference.ResolvedMidiDataProvider
member.summary: For internal use during loading: the resolved MidiDataProvider instance. Not serialized.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTempoMarker
member.summary: Represents a serializable tempo marker for saving and loading composition projects.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTempoMarker.Time
member.summary: Gets or sets the time of the tempo marker.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTempoMarker.BeatsPerMinute
member.summary: Gets or sets the tempo in beats per minute.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrack
member.summary: Serializable representation of a for project files. This DTO captures the essential properties of an audio track for persistence, including its segments and track-level settings.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrack.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the track.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrack.Segments
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of s contained within this track. These segments define the audio content and arrangement on the track.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrack.Settings
member.summary: Gets or sets the configurable settings for this track, such as master volume, pan, and mute/solo states.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings
member.summary: Represents the configurable settings for a Track, suitable for data transfer and serialization.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.Volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the master volume level for the track. A value of 1.0f is normal volume.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.Pan
member.summary: Gets or sets the stereo pan position for the entire track. A value of -1.0f means full left, 0.0f means center, and 1.0f means full right.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.IsMuted
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is muted. If true, the track will produce no audio output.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.IsSoloed
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is soloed. If true, only this track (and other soloed tracks) will produce audio output in the composition. Non-soloed tracks will be muted.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is enabled for playback. If false, the track will produce silence, regardless of mute/solo settings.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets the chain of sound modifiers (effects) to be applied to this track. These are represented as DTOs to carry their serializable data.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets the chain of audio analyzers to process this track's mixed audio. These are represented as DTOs to carry their serializable data.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Persistence.ProjectTrackSettings.MidiModifiers
member.summary: Gets the chain of MIDI modifiers (effects) to be applied to this track. These are represented as DTOs to carry their serializable data.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.TempoMarker
member.summary: Represents a tempo change at a specific point in the composition's timeline.
member.param: The absolute time on the composition timeline where the tempo change occurs.
The new tempo in beats per minute.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TempoMarker.#ctor(System.TimeSpan,System.Double)
member.summary: Represents a tempo change at a specific point in the composition's timeline.

The new tempo in beats per minute.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TempoMarker.Time
member.summary: The absolute time on the composition timeline where the tempo change occurs.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TempoMarker.BeatsPerMinute
