member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.Track
member.summary: Represents a single audio track within a composition, containing a collection of audio segments and applying track-level settings like volume, pan, mute, and solo.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the track.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Segments
member.summary: Gets the list of s contained within this track. Segments are automatically sorted by their timeline start time.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Settings
member.summary: Gets or sets the settings applied to this track, such as volume, pan, mute, and solo.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.ParentComposition
member.summary: Gets or sets the parent composition to which this track belongs.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.#ctor(System.String,SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the track. Defaults to "Track".
Optional initial settings for the track. If null, default settings are used.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.MarkDirty
member.summary: Marks the parent composition as dirty (having unsaved changes).

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.AddSegment(SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment)
member.summary: Adds an to the track. The track's segments are re-sorted after adding.
member.param: The audio segment to add.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.RemoveSegment(SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Removes an from the track.
member.param: The audio segment to remove.

If true, all segments on the track that start at or after the removed segment's original start time will be shifted earlier by the duration of the removed segment. This closes the gap created by removal.
member.returns: True if the segment was successfully removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.InsertSegmentAt(SoundFlow.Editing.AudioSegment,System.TimeSpan,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Inserts an into the track at a specified time.
member.param: The audio segment to insert.
The timeline point at which to insert the segment.
If true, all segments on the track that start at or after the insertion point will be shifted later by the duration of the inserted segment. This makes space for the new segment.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.SortSegments
member.summary: Sorts the internal list of segments by their . This method is called automatically after adding segments.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.CalculateDuration
member.summary: Calculates the total duration of the track based on the latest ending segment.
member.returns: A representing the total duration of the track.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Render(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan,System.Span{System.Single},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders this track's audio output for a given time range into a provided buffer. Segments are processed, and their output is mixed together, then track-level volume and pan are applied.
member.param: The global timeline start time for the rendering operation.
The duration of audio to render from the track.
The span to fill with the rendered audio samples. This buffer will be cleared before rendering.
The desired sample rate for the output audio.
The desired number of channels for the output audio.
member.returns: The number of samples actually written to the output buffer.
member.exception: Thrown if an invalid channel value is encountered during pan calculation.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Max(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Compares two values and returns the greater one.
member.param: The first TimeSpan to compare.
The second TimeSpan to compare.
member.returns: The larger of the two TimeSpans.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.Track.Min(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Compares two values and returns the smaller one.
member.param: The first TimeSpan to compare.
The second TimeSpan to compare.
member.returns: The smaller of the two TimeSpans.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings
member.summary: Represents the configurable settings for a or , controlling its overall playback characteristics such as volume, pan, and mute/solo states.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.ParentTrack
member.summary: Gets or sets the parent that owns these settings. This is used internally to propagate dirty state for audio tracks.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.Modifiers
member.summary: Gets the chain of sound modifiers (effects) to be applied to this track. These are applied after all segments on the track are mixed.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.Analyzers
member.summary: Gets the chain of audio analyzers to process this track's mixed audio. Analyzers process the audio after all track modifiers have been applied.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.MidiModifiers
member.summary: Gets the chain of MIDI modifiers (effects) to be applied to this track in real-time. This is only applicable for .

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.Volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the master volume level for the track. A value of 1.0f is normal volume.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.Pan
member.summary: Gets or sets the stereo pan position for the entire track. A value of -1.0f means full left, 0.0f means center, and 1.0f means full right.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.IsMuted
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is muted. If true, the track will produce no audio output.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.IsSoloed
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is soloed. If true, only this track (and other soloed tracks) will produce audio output in the composition. Non-soloed tracks will be muted.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.IsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the track is enabled for playback. If false, the track will produce silence, regardless of mute/solo settings.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.Clone
member.summary: Creates a shallow clone of the current instance. The cloned instance will have the same scalar property values and copies of the Modifiers and Analyzers lists, but its will be null.
member.returns: A new object with the same property values.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.MarkDirty
member.summary: Marks the parent track as dirty, propagating the change up the hierarchy to the composition.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.AddModifier(SoundFlow.Abstracts.SoundModifier)
member.summary: Adds a to the end of the track's modifier chain.
member.param: The modifier to add. Cannot be null.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.RemoveModifier(SoundFlow.Abstracts.SoundModifier)
member.summary: Removes a specific from the track's modifier chain.
member.param: The modifier to remove.
member.returns: True if the modifier was found and removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.ReorderModifier(SoundFlow.Abstracts.SoundModifier,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reorders a within the track's modifier chain to a new index.
member.param: The modifier to reorder.
The zero-based index where the modifier should be moved to.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.AddAnalyzer(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioAnalyzer)
member.summary: Adds an to the end of the track's analyzer chain.
member.param: The analyzer to add. Cannot be null.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.RemoveAnalyzer(SoundFlow.Abstracts.AudioAnalyzer)
member.summary: Removes a specific from the track's analyzer chain.
member.param: The analyzer to remove.
member.returns: True if the analyzer was found and removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.AddMidiModifier(SoundFlow.Midi.Abstracts.MidiModifier)
member.summary: Adds a to the end of the track's MIDI modifier chain.
member.param: The MIDI modifier to add. Cannot be null.
member.exception: Thrown if is null.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.RemoveMidiModifier(SoundFlow.Midi.Abstracts.MidiModifier)
member.summary: Removes a specific from the track's MIDI modifier chain.
member.param: The MIDI modifier to remove.
member.returns: True if the modifier was found and removed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Editing.TrackSettings.ReorderMidiModifier(SoundFlow.Midi.Abstracts.MidiModifier,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reorders a within the track's modifier chain to a new index.
member.param: The MIDI modifier to reorder.
The zero-based index where the modifier should be moved to.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Interfaces.MappingScale
member.summary: Defines the scaling behavior for a controllable parameter when mapped to a normalized input (e.g., 0.0 to 1.0).

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Interfaces.MappingScale.Linear
member.summary: The parameter responds linearly to the input. Suitable for parameters like volume, pan, or wet/dry mix.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Interfaces.MappingScale.Logarithmic
member.summary: The parameter responds logarithmically to the input. This provides finer control at the lower end of the range and is ideal for frequency-based parameters like filter cutoff to match human perception.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute
member.summary: An attribute used to decorate properties on `IMidiMappable` objects, exposing them for real-time control and providing metadata for user interfaces and the mapping system.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute.DisplayName
member.summary: Gets the user-friendly display name for the parameter.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute.MinValue
member.summary: Gets the minimum valid value for the parameter.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute.MaxValue
member.summary: Gets the maximum valid value for the parameter.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute.Scale
member.summary: Gets the scaling curve that should be used when mapping a normalized value (0.0 to 1.0) to this parameter.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ControllableParameterAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Double,System.Double,SoundFlow.Interfaces.MappingScale)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The user-friendly display name for the parameter.
The minimum valid value for the parameter.
The maximum valid value for the parameter.
The scaling curve to use for mapping. Defaults to Linear.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory
member.summary: Defines a factory for creating custom and instances. Implement this interface to add support for new audio formats or to provide alternative implementations for existing formats.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.FactoryId
member.summary: Gets a unique, lowercase string identifier for this factory implementation. It is recommended to use a namespace-qualified name to avoid collisions, e.g., "mycompany.customflacfactory". This ID is used for unregistering and re-prioritizing codecs.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.SupportedFormatIds
member.summary: Gets a collection of unique, lowercase string identifiers for the audio formats this factory supports. For example, a factory for FFmpeg might return ["mp3", "aac", "ogg", "opus"]. A factory for a specific library would return a single format, like ["flac"].

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.Priority
member.summary: Gets the default priority of this factory. This priority is used upon initial registration but can be overridden at runtime using . When multiple factories are registered for the same format, the one with the highest priority number will be tried first. Built-in engine codecs use a low priority (e.g., 0).

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.CreateDecoder(System.IO.Stream,System.String,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Creates a new decoder instance for one of the supported formats.
member.param: The input stream containing the audio data.
The specific format identifier being requested (e.g., "mp3", "flac"). This allows a multi-format factory to know which decoder to create.
The audio format parameters discovered by the engine.
member.returns: A valid instance on success, or null if this factory fails to create a decoder for the given stream.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.TryCreateDecoder(System.IO.Stream,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat@,System.Nullable{SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat})
member.summary: Attempts to create a decoder by probing the given stream directly. This method is called by the engine when the audio format is unknown.
member.param: The input stream containing audio data. The stream must be seekable.
When this method returns, contains the audio format detected by the decoder if successful.
An optional hint for the desired output audio format. The factory should attempt to produce this format if possible.
member.returns: A valid instance on success, or null if the factory cannot handle the stream.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ICodecFactory.CreateEncoder(System.IO.Stream,System.String,SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Creates a new encoder instance for one of the supported formats.
member.param: The output stream to write encoded audio to.
The specific format identifier being requested (e.g., "wav").
The audio format parameters for the raw audio to be encoded.
member.returns: A valid instance on success, or null if encoding is not supported or fails.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the data source supports seeking.

member.summary: Gets the detailed format and tag information read from the source file. This will be null if the provider is not sourced from a file with metadata (e.g., raw data, microphone).

member.summary: Reads the specified number of audio bytes into the given buffer asynchronously.
member.param: The buffer to write the bytes to.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous read operation. The task result contains the number of bytes actually read. May be less than the requested number if the end of the data is reached.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer
member.summary: Defines the interface for a sound player component.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.State
member.summary: Gets the current playback state of the sound player.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.DataProvider
member.summary: Gets the data provider that supplies audio for playback.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.IsLooping
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the sound player is currently looping the audio.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.PlaybackSpeed
member.summary: Gets or sets the playback speed of the sound player. A value of 1.0 represents normal speed. Values greater than 1.0 increase the speed, and values less than 1.0 decrease it.
member.remarks: The current implementation uses linear interpolation which may affect the pitch.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Volume
member.summary: Gets or sets the volume of the sound player

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Time
member.summary: Gets the current playback time in seconds, relative to the beginning of the audio.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Duration
member.summary: Gets the total duration of the audio in seconds.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.LoopStartSamples
member.summary: Gets the loop start point in samples.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.LoopEndSamples
member.summary: Gets the loop end point in samples. -1 indicates loop to the natural end of the audio.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.LoopStartSeconds
member.summary: Gets the loop start point in seconds.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.LoopEndSeconds
member.summary: Gets the loop end point in seconds. -1 indicates loop to the natural end of the audio.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Play
member.summary: Starts or resumes playback of the audio from the current position. If the player is already playing, calling this method may have no effect. If the player is stopped, playback starts from the beginning. If the player is paused, playback resumes from the paused position.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Pause
member.summary: Pauses playback of the audio at the current position. If the player is already paused or stopped, calling this method may have no effect. Playback can be resumed from the paused position by calling .

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Stop
member.summary: Stops playback of the audio and resets the playback position to the beginning. If the player is already stopped, calling this method has no effect. After stopping, playback can be restarted from the beginning by calling .

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Seek(System.TimeSpan,System.IO.SeekOrigin)
member.summary: Seeks to a specific time in the audio playback using TimeSpan.
member.param: The time to seek to as a TimeSpan, relative to the beginning of the audio.

SeekOrigin - specifies how to calculate the offset
SeekOrigin.Begin: Beginning of the audio stream
SeekOrigin.Current: Current position of the audio stream
SeekOrigin.End: Duration of the audio stream (offset has to be negative)

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Seek(System.Single)
member.summary: Seeks to a specific time in the audio playback.
member.param: The time in seconds to seek to, relative to the beginning of the audio.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.Seek(System.Int32)
member.summary: Seeks to a specific sample offset in the audio playback.
member.param: The sample offset to seek to, relative to the beginning of the audio data.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.SetLoopPoints(System.Single,System.Nullable{System.Single})
member.summary: Sets the loop points for the sound player in seconds.
member.param: The loop start time in seconds. Must be non-negative.
The loop end time in seconds, optional. Use -1 or null to loop to the natural end of the audio. Must be greater than or equal to startTime, or -1.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.SetLoopPoints(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the loop points for the sound player in samples.
member.param: The loop start sample. Must be non-negative.
The loop end sample, optional. Use -1 or null to loop to the natural end of the audio. Must be greater than or equal to startSample, or -1.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Interfaces.ISoundPlayer.SetLoopPoints(System.TimeSpan,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Sets the loop points for the sound player using TimeSpan.
member.param: The loop start time as a TimeSpan. Must be non-negative.
The loop end time as a TimeSpan, optional. Use null to loop to the natural end of the audio. Must be greater than or equal to startTime, or null.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.DurationAccuracy
member.summary: Specifies the desired accuracy vs. speed for duration calculation.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.DurationAccuracy.FastEstimate
member.summary: Use the bitrate of the first frame to estimate the duration. This method is faster but may not be accurate for VBR files.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.DurationAccuracy.AccurateScan
member.summary: Scan the entire file to accurately calculate the duration. This method is slower but provides more accurate results for VBR files.

member.summary: Configuration options to control the parsing behavior of the AudioReader.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.ReadOptions.ReadTags
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to parse metadata tags (artist, title, etc.). Default is true.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.ReadOptions.ReadAlbumArt
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to parse embedded album art. This setting is ignored if ReadTags is false. Default is false.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.ReadOptions.ReadCueSheet
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to parse embedded CUE sheets. Default is false.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.ReadOptions.DurationAccuracy
member.summary: Gets or sets the desired accuracy for duration calculation, especially for VBR files. Default is AccurateScan.

member.summary: Constant bitrate (CBR) mode. Indicates that the audio bitrate is constant.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.BitrateMode.VBR
member.summary: Variable bitrate (VBR) mode. Indicates that the audio bitrate varies.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.BitrateMode.ABR
member.summary: Average bitrate (ABR) mode. Indicates that the audio bitrate is averaged.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.BitrateMode.Unknown
member.summary: Unknown bitrate mode. Indicates that the bitrate mode is unknown.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.SoundFormatInfo.BitsPerSample
member.summary: The number of bits per sample (e.g., 16, 24). Not applicable for lossy formats (0).

member.summary: The average bitrate in bits per second (bps).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.SoundFormatInfo.BitrateMode
member.summary: The bitrate mode (CBR, VBR, etc.).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Metadata.Models.SoundFormatInfo.IsLossless
member.summary: Indicates if the audio codec is lossless.

member.summary: The embedded cue sheet defining tracks within the file. Null if not read or not present.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter
member.summary: Implements a digital biquad filter, allowing for various filter types such as LowPass, HighPass, BandPass, and Notch.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.FilterType
member.summary: Defines the different types of filters available.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.FilterType.LowPass
member.summary: Allows frequencies below the cutoff frequency to pass, attenuating frequencies above it.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.FilterType.HighPass
member.summary: Allows frequencies above the cutoff frequency to pass, attenuating frequencies below it.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.FilterType.BandPass
member.summary: Allows frequencies around the cutoff frequency to pass, attenuating frequencies further away.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.FilterType.Notch
member.summary: Attenuates frequencies around the cutoff frequency, allowing frequencies further away to pass.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.Format
member.summary: Gets or sets the audio format.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type of filter. Changing the filter type recalculates the filter coefficients.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.CutoffFrequency
member.summary: Gets or sets the cutoff frequency of the filter in Hertz. This frequency determines the point at which the filter starts to attenuate the signal. Changing the cutoff frequency recalculates the filter coefficients.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.Resonance
member.summary: Gets or sets the resonance of the filter, a value between 0 and 1. Higher resonance values emphasize frequencies around the cutoff frequency, potentially leading to self-oscillation in some filter types. Changing the resonance recalculates the filter coefficients. Resonance is clamped between 0.01 and 0.99 to prevent instability.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.#ctor(SoundFlow.Structs.AudioFormat)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with default settings and calculates initial filter coefficients.
member.param: The audio format containing channels and sample rate and sample format

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.Filter.CalculateCoefficients
member.summary: Calculates the biquad filter coefficients based on the current , , and parameters. This method uses standard formulas for digital biquad filter coefficient calculation and normalizes the coefficients.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Modifiers.ResamplerModifier
member.summary: A real-time resampling modifier that changes the playback speed and pitch of an audio signal. This is achieved by changing the number of samples in the audio stream, which affects both its duration and perceived pitch.
member.remarks: Due to the nature of resampling, this modifier introduces a small amount of latency as it needs to buffer incoming audio to perform its calculations.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.ResamplerModifier.ResampleFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the resampling factor. Values greater than 1.0 speed up playback and increase pitch. Values less than 1.0 slow down playback and decrease pitch. A value of 1.0 results in no change.
member.exception: Thrown if the value is less than or equal to zero.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.ResamplerModifier.#ctor(System.Single)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specific factor.
member.param: The initial resampling factor. Defaults to 1.0 (no change).

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.ResamplerModifier.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class by specifying source and target sample rates. The resampling factor is calculated as `targetRate / sourceRate`.
member.param: The original sample rate of the audio.
The target sample rate to resample to, which determines the new speed and pitch.

The gain of the boost in decibels.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.TrebleBoosterModifier.BoostGainDb
member.summary: Gets or sets the gain of the treble boost in decibels.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.TrebleBoosterModifier.Cutoff

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier
member.summary: An advanced vocal extraction effect that supports Mono, Stereo, and Surround formats.

remarks.para: For Stereo pairs (e.g., L/R), this uses Spatial Isolation (Mid-Side subtraction) to remove instruments panned to the sides.
For Mono or isolated channels (e.g., Center in 5.1), it falls back to Spectral Gating and Bandpass filtering to suppress noise and non-vocal frequencies.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.MakeupGain
member.summary: Gain multiplier to compensate for windowing and subtraction loss.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.StereoPenalty
member.summary: The penalty factor for stereo width. Higher values (e.g., 2.0 - 3.0) more aggressively remove instruments panned to the sides.

member.name: F:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.BackgroundThreshold
member.summary: The spectral gating threshold. Frequencies quieter than this percentage of the frame's peak volume are suppressed.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.MinFrequency
member.summary: The lower bound of the frequency range to preserve (in Hz). Set to ~100Hz to keep the fundamental vocal frequencies (warmth) while cutting sub-bass.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.MaxFrequency
member.summary: The upper bound of the frequency range to preserve (in Hz).

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.FftSize
member.summary: The size of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) window.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.HopSize
member.summary: The hop size of the FFT.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ChannelState
member.summary: Holds the processing state for a single audio channel.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ChannelState.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Holds the processing state for a single audio channel.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructs a new instance of .
member.param: The audio sample rate in Hz.
The lower bound of the frequency range. Defaults to 100Hz to preserve vocal body.
The upper bound of the frequency range. Defaults to 10000Hz.
The size of the FFT window. Defaults to 4096 for better bass resolution.
The hop size of the FFT. Defaults to 1024.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ProcessPair(SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ChannelState,SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ChannelState)
member.summary: Processes two channels simultaneously using Spatial Isolation (Mid-Side). Best for L/R pairs in Stereo or Quad setups.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ProcessMono(SoundFlow.Modifiers.VocalExtractorModifier.ChannelState)
member.summary: Processes a single channel using Bandpass and Spectral Gating only. Used for Mono inputs, or the Center/LFE channels in Surround setups.

member.name: T:SoundFlow.Utils.MathHelper
member.summary: Helper methods for common math operations.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Utils.MathHelper.EnableAvx
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use AVX (Advanced Vector Extensions) instructions if the hardware supports them. Defaults to true.
member.remarks: Setting this to false will prevent the use of AVX, and the implementation will fall back to SSE or scalar code, even on AVX-capable hardware.

member.name: P:SoundFlow.Utils.MathHelper.EnableSse
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use SSE (Streaming SIMD Extensions) instructions if the hardware supports them. Defaults to true.
member.remarks: Setting this to false will prevent the use of SSE, and the implementation will fall back to scalar code, even on SSE-capable hardware. This also affects AVX routines that may use SSE for specific operations.

member.name: M:SoundFlow.Utils.MathHelper.ResampleLinear(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Resamples an array of audio data using linear interpolation. This is a high-performance method for offline, one-shot resampling that affects both speed and pitch.
member.param: The raw audio samples to resample. The samples should be interleaved for multi-channel audio.
The number of audio channels in the input data.
The original sample rate of the input data in Hz.
The desired sample rate for the output data in Hz.
member.returns: A new array containing the resampled audio data.

member.param: The complex data array. Must be a power of 2 in length.

member.summary: Scalar recursive implementation of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).

member.summary: AVX-accelerated iterative implementation of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT).
